THE FULL STORY

One kid, one printer, and a workshop that keeps changing.

Founder & Maker
AGE 11 · EST. 2025

I'm Faizi Mohamed Hafiz, and Forge Core Labs started with a printer, a pile of failed prints, and a lot of patience I didn't know I had.

It began the way most of these things do: watching a 3D printer lay down its first layer and not being able to look away. The first few prints were rough — stringing everywhere, colours bleeding into each other, supports that took longer to remove than the print took to make. None of that went in the bin, though. Every failed print taught me something about the next one.

The name tags and keychains you see in the shop today went through dozens of versions before they looked right. Getting multicolour prints to swap cleanly, mid-print, without ruining the piece, took a lot of trial and error — and a lot of wasted filament. Bookmarks and fidget keychains came later, once I'd learned enough about how interlocking parts print as one piece without fusing together.

Forge Core Labs isn't a factory and it isn't dropshipped from somewhere else. It's one printer, running in a home workshop, with every file designed from scratch before it's allowed anywhere near a print bed. When something doesn't work, it gets redesigned, not shipped anyway.

Every year the plan is the same: learn something new, get better at the craft, and make something a little more ambitious than the year before. This year that's meant multicolour AMS prints and a proper partnership with a local shop. Next year, who knows.

Thanks for reading this far — and for supporting a one-person workshop that's still figuring a lot of this out as it goes.

HOW IT STARTED

The journey so far.

A few of the milestones that got Forge Core Labs to where it is today.

STEP 01

First print

Watched a printer lay its first layer and got hooked immediately.

STEP 02

Learning to design

Started modelling simple shapes, then worked up to interlocking parts.

STEP 03

Multicolour prints

Got the AMS working reliably for clean colour swaps mid-print.

STEP 04

First sale

Sold the first batch of name keychains — and reinvested it all in filament.

STEP 05

Shop partnership

Forge Core Labs landed a shelf in a local shop, with its own display.

WHAT MATTERS HERE

The values behind the workshop.

No shortcuts

If a print doesn't come out right, it gets redesigned — not shipped anyway.

Show the process

Failed prints and redraws are part of the story, not something to hide.

Get better every year

New skills, new designs, and a workshop that keeps evolving.
